info.title: Hebcal REST APIs
info.description:
Hebcal.com publishes a family of free, public Jewish calendar / Hebrew date / zmanim REST APIs. All endpoints support HTTP and HTTPS (HTTP/2), gzip and brotli compression, and HTTP Keep-Alive.

Content generated by these APIs is licensed under a [Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).

Reference documentation:
- [Jewish Calendar API](https://www.hebcal.com/home/195/jewish-calendar-rest-api)
- [Hebrew Date Converter API](https://www.hebcal.com/home/219/hebrew-date-converter-rest-api)
- [Zmanim API](https://www.hebcal.com/home/1663/zmanim-halachic-times-api)
- [Leyning (Torah Reading) API](https://www.hebcal.com/home/4277/leyning-torah-reading-api)
- [Yahrzeit + Anniversary API](https://www.hebcal.com/home/1705/yahrzeit-anniversary-api)
- [Assur Melacha (Work Forbidden) API](https://www.hebcal.com/home/4984/assur-melacha-work-forbidden-api)
- [Specifying a location for Jewish calendar APIs](https://www.hebcal.com/home/4912/specifying-a-location-for-jewish-calendar-apis)
- [Event language support](https://www.hebcal.com/home/4534/jewish-calendar-event-language-support)

tags.name: Jewish Calendar
tags.description: Jewish holidays, Parashat ha-Shavua, candle lighting times, daily learning, Hebrew dates and more.

tags.name: Hebrew Date Converter
tags.description: Convert between Gregorian and Hebrew dates (single date or range).

tags.name: Zmanim
tags.description: Halachic (sun-based) times for any city or coordinates.

tags.name: Leyning
tags.description: Torah Reading details: full kriyah aliyot, haftarah, and triennial cycle.

tags.name: Yahrzeit
tags.description: Personal calendar of yahrzeit (memorial) dates, Hebrew birthdays, and Hebrew anniversaries.

tags.name: Assur Melacha
tags.description: Determine whether a given moment falls within a melacha (work-forbidden) period such as Shabbat or a Yom Tov.

get.description:
Returns Jewish holidays, candle lighting times, Parashat ha-Shavua, daily learning schedules and more.

Date range may be specified by `year` (with optional `month`, `ny`, `yt`) **or** by both `start` and `end`.

parameters.name: year
parameters.description: Gregorian or Hebrew year (4-digit). Use `now` for the current year.

parameters.name: yt
parameters.description: Year type: `G` for Gregorian (default) or `H` for Hebrew.

parameters.name: month
parameters.description: Single Gregorian month 1–12, or `x` for entire year (default).

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Gregorian start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Use with `end` instead of `year`.

parameters.name: min
parameters.description: Minor holidays (Tu BiShvat, Lag B'Omer, etc.).

parameters.name: mod
parameters.description: Modern holidays (Yom HaAtzmaut, Yom HaShoah, etc.).

parameters.name: yto
parameters.description: Yom Tov only. Suppresses all other event options.

parameters.name: leyning
parameters.description: Include full Torah reading details (default `on` when `s=on`).

parameters.name: D
parameters.description: Show Hebrew date for dates that have an event.

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Show Hebrew date for entire date range.

parameters.name: molad
parameters.description: Molad — moment of new moon for each month.

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: Diaspora (`off`, default) or Israel (`on`) holiday/Torah schedule.

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: Include candle lighting times. Requires a location (see location parameters below).

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Minutes before sunset for candle lighting. Defaults: 18 (most cities), 40 (Jerusalem), 30 (Haifa, Zikhron Ya'akov).

parameters.name: m
parameters.description: Fixed minutes after sundown for Havdalah. `0` disables Havdalah.

parameters.name: dpa
parameters.description: Pirkei Avot (on Shabbatot between Pesach and Rosh Hashanah)

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: JSONP callback function name (alphanumeric, periods, underscores).

may2015LosAngeles.summary: Major holidays for May 2015 in Los Angeles

get.summary: Convert between Gregorian and Hebrew dates
get.description:
Four modes are supported:

1. **Gregorian → Hebrew (single date):** pass `g2h=1` with `date=YYYY-MM-DD` (or `gy`/`gm`/`gd`).
2. **Gregorian → Hebrew (range):** pass `g2h=1` with `start` and `end` (max 180 days; `cfg=json` only).
3. **Hebrew → Gregorian (single date):** pass `h2g=1` with `hy`, `hm`, `hd`.
4. **Hebrew → Gregorian (range):** pass `h2g=1` with `hy`, `hm`, `hd`, `ndays` (2–180; `cfg=json` only).

Month name (`hm`) accepts: `Nisan`, `Iyyar`/`Iyar`, `Sivan`, `Tamuz`/`Tammuz`, `Av`, `Elul`, `Tishrei`, `Cheshvan`, `Kislev`, `Tevet`/`Teves`, `Sh'vat`/`Shvat`, `Adar`/`Adar1`/`Adar I`/`Adar2`/`Adar II`. Hebrew UTF-8 names are also accepted. In a leap year `Adar` is `Adar2`; in a non-leap year all three Adars are equivalent.

parameters.name: g2h
parameters.description: Set to `1` for Gregorian→Hebrew conversion. Mutually exclusive with `h2g`.

parameters.name: h2g
parameters.description: Set to `1` for Hebrew→Gregorian conversion. Mutually exclusive with `g2h`.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Gregorian date (YYYY-MM-DD). Used in single-date g2h mode as an alternative to `gy`/`gm`/`gd`.

parameters.name: gs
parameters.description: After-sunset designation. When `on`, returns the Hebrew date of the next day (sunset starts the new Hebrew day).

parameters.name: ndays
parameters.description: Number of days to convert in h2g range mode (2–180).

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Range start date YYYY-MM-DD (g2h range mode).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Range end date YYYY-MM-DD (g2h range mode). Truncated to 180 days from `start`.

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: When `on`, use Israel holiday schedule when listing events for a date.

parameters.name: strict
parameters.description: Return HTTP 400 for invalid input dates instead of redirecting to a fallback.

get.summary: Halachic times for a date or range; or check whether a moment is assur bemelacha
get.description:
Two modes:

1. **Zmanim mode (default):** Returns sun-based halachic times for a single `date` or a `start`/`end` range.
2. **Assur Melacha mode:** Pass `im=1` and (optionally) `dt=<ISO 8601 datetime>` to determine whether that moment falls within a melacha (work-forbidden) period such as Shabbat or a Yom Tov. The check uses solar depression of 8.5°.

A location is required (see location parameters).

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Single Gregorian date YYYY-MM-DD. If neither `date` nor `start/end` are specified, defaults to today at the location.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Range end date YYYY-MM-DD. Capped at 180 days from `start`.

parameters.name: sec
parameters.description: If `1`, output time-of-day with seconds precision (otherwise rounded to the nearest minute).

parameters.name: im
parameters.description: Assur Melacha mode. When set, returns `status.isAssurBemlacha` instead of zmanim.

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description: Used with `im=1`. Specific moment to check, in ISO 8601 format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ`. Timezone offset can be `Z` or `±HH:MM`. URL-encode `+` as `%2b`. If omitted, uses the current time at the location.

get.summary: Torah readings for a date or range
get.description: Returns full kriyah aliyot, haftarah, and (optionally) triennial cycle readings. Use either a single `date` or both `start` and `end` (capped at 180 days).

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Single Gregorian date YYYY-MM-DD. Equivalent to `start=date&end=date`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Range end date YYYY-MM-DD. Capped at 180 days from `start`.

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: Diaspora (`off`, default) or Israel (`on`) Torah reading schedule.

parameters.name: triennial
parameters.description: Include triennial cycle aliyot. Defaults to `on`. (Triennial only available for Hebrew year 5745 and later.)

post.summary: Yahrzeit, Hebrew birthday and Hebrew anniversary calendar
post.description:
Calculates the Hebrew anniversary of an event (death, birth, marriage, etc.) for a number of years.

**HTTPS is required.** The body must be `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.

Provide one or more event records using a numeric suffix `1`, `2`, `3`… for each person/event. For each record, supply either Gregorian date fields (`yX`, `mX`, `dX`, optional `sX`) **or** Hebrew date fields (`hyX`, `hmX`, `hdX`).

Example (curl):
```
curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \\
  --data-raw 'cfg=json&v=yahrzeit&n1=Person1&t1=Birthday&d1=15&m1=4&y1=1983&s1=on&years=3' \\
  https://www.hebcal.com/yahrzeit
```

Geonameid.name: geonameid
Geonameid.description:
GeoNames.org numeric city identifier. The Hebcal database supports approximately 150,000 world cities with population ≥ 1,000 (refreshed annually from the [`cities1000.zip`](https://download.geonames.org/export/dump/) feed).

**Examples:**
- `3448439` — São Paulo, BR
- `5128581` — New York, US
- `5368361` — Los Angeles, US
- `281184` — Jerusalem, IL

Zip.name: zip
Zip.description: 5-digit US ZIP code (e.g. `90210`). ZIP+4 input is truncated to the first 5 digits.

City.name: city
City.description: **Deprecated.** Legacy Hebcal city identifier (e.g. `GB-London`). Case-insensitive. Prefer `geonameid`.

Latitude.name: latitude
Latitude.description: Decimal latitude (-90.0 to 90.0). Must be combined with `longitude` and `tzid`.

Longitude.name: longitude
Longitude.description: Decimal longitude (-180.0 to 180.0). Must be combined with `latitude` and `tzid`.

Tzid.name: tzid
Tzid.description: TZ database identifier such as `America/New_York` or `Asia/Jerusalem`. Required when using `latitude`/`longitude`.

Elev.name: elev
Elev.description: Elevation in meters (1–9000). Only honored when `ue=on`.

Lg.name: lg
Lg.description:
Event-title locale. Default `s` (Sephardic transliteration).

| Code | Language |
|------|----------|
| `s` | Sephardic transliterations (default) |
| `a` | Ashkenazic transliterations |
| `h` / `he` | Hebrew |
| `he-x-NoNikud` | Hebrew without diacritics |
| `sh` | Sephardic transliteration with Hebrew |
| `ah` | Ashkenazic transliteration with Hebrew |
| `ashkenazi_standard` | Ashkenazic transliteration (standard) |
| `ashkenazi_litvish` | Ashkenazic transliteration (Litvish) |
| `ashkenazi_poylish` | Ashkenazic transliteration (Poylish) |
| `ashkenazi_romanian` | Ashkenazic transliteration (Romanian variant) |
| `de` | German |
| `es` | Spanish |
| `fi` | Finnish |
| `fr` | French |
| `hu` | Hungarian |
| `nl` | Dutch |
| `pl` | Polish |
| `pt` | Portuguese |
| `ro` | Romanian |
| `ru` | Russian |
| `uk` | Ukrainian |

OnOff.description: Boolean flag. Use `on` or `1` to enable, `off` or `0` to disable.

date.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the response was generated.
Either `YYYY-MM-DD` (untimed) or full ISO 8601 with timezone (timed events like candle lighting).

events.description: Holiday/parsha names that fall on this Hebrew date.

hdates.description: Object mapping each Gregorian date (YYYY-MM-DD) to its Hebrew conversion.

ZmanimTimes.description: ISO 8601 timestamps with timezone offset. For range queries, each property is an object mapping `YYYY-MM-DD` → time. Times calculated using NOAA solar algorithms (expect 1-2 minute variation from other sources).

chatzotNight.description: Chatzot HaLailah — sunset plus 6 halachic hours.

alotHaShachar.description: Alot haShachar — sun is 16.1° below horizon in the morning.

alosBaalHatanya.description: Alot HaShachar (Baal Hatanya) — sun 16.9° below horizon in the morning.

dawn.description: Civil dawn — sun is 6° below horizon.

sunrise.description: Sunrise (upper edge of sun, 0.833° above horizon).

plagHaMincha.description: Plag HaMincha — sunrise plus 10.75 halachic hours.

sunset.description: Sunset (upper edge of sun, 0.833° below horizon).

beinHaShmashos.description: Twilight — 13.5 minutes prior to tzeit (sun 7.083° below).

dusk.description: Civil dusk — sun is 6° below horizon.

tzeit72min.description: Nightfall — fixed 72 minutes after sunset (Rabbeinu Tam).

tzaisBaalHatanya.description: Nightfall (Baal Hatanya) — sun 6° below western geometric horizon after sunset.

isAssurBemlacha.description: True if the moment is within a melacha (work-forbidden) period.

fullkriyah.description: Full kriyah aliyot keyed by aliyah number / `M` for Maftir.

triennial.description: Triennial cycle aliyot (when `triennial` is on and Hebrew year ≥ 5745).

triYear.description: Year (1, 2, or 3) within the triennial cycle.

years.description: Number of Hebrew years to compute (default 20). Use `years=1` for a single year.

start.description: Starting Hebrew year (defaults to current Hebrew year).

end.description: Ending Hebrew year (inclusive). Defaults to `start + years`.

hebdate.description: Append Hebrew date to event titles.

i.description: Yizkor schedule: `off` for Diaspora, `on` for Israel.

hdp.description: If `1`, include `heDateParts` field on all-day items.

hy1.description: Hebrew year for record #1 (alternative to Gregorian).

hm1.description: Hebrew month for record #1 (Hebrew UTF-8 or transliterated).

additionalProperties.description: For multiple records, repeat the per-record fields with incrementing suffixes (e.g. `y2`, `m2`, `d2`, `t2`, `n2`, `s2`, or `hy2`, `hm2`, `hd2`) for record #2, and so on.
